member.name: P:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.CrossFirebaseAnalytics.IsSupported
member.summary: Gets if the plugin is supported on the current platform.

member.name: T:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.IFirebaseAnalytics
member.summary: The top level Firebase Analytics singleton that provides methods for logging events and setting user properties.

member.name: M:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.IFirebaseAnalytics.GetAppInstanceIdAsync
member.summary: Returns the unique ID for this instance of the application or null if ConsentType.analyticsStorage has been set to ConsentStatus.denied.

member.name: M:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.IFirebaseAnalytics.LogEvent(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Logs an app event. The event can have up to 25 parameters. Events with the same name must have the same parameters. Up to 500 event names are supported. Using predefined events and/or parameters is recommended for optimal reporting.
member.param: The name of the event. Should contain 1 to 40 alphanumeric characters or underscores. The name must start with an alphabetic character. Some event names are reserved. See FIREventNames.h for the list of reserved event names. The “firebase_”, “google_”, and “ga_” prefixes are reserved and should not be used. Note that event names are case-sensitive and that logging two events whose names differ only in case will result in two distinct events. To manually log screen view events, use the screen_view event name.

The dictionary of event parameters. Passing null indicates that the event has no parameters. Parameter names can be up to 40 characters long and must start with an alphabetic character and contain only alphanumeric characters and underscores. Only NSString and NSNumber (signed 64-bit integer and 64-bit floating-point number) parameter types are supported. NSString parameter values can be up to 100 characters long. The “firebase_”, “google_”, and “ga_” prefixes are reserved and should not be used for parameter names.

member.name: M:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.IFirebaseAnalytics.LogEvent(System.String,System.ValueTuple{System.String,System.Object}[])
member.summary: Logs an app event. The event can have up to 25 parameters. Events with the same name must have the same parameters. Up to 500 event names are supported. Using predefined events and/or parameters is recommended for optimal reporting.
member.param: The name of the event. Should contain 1 to 40 alphanumeric characters or underscores. The name must start with an alphabetic character. Some event names are reserved. See FIREventNames.h for the list of reserved event names. The “firebase_”, “google_”, and “ga_” prefixes are reserved and should not be used. Note that event names are case-sensitive and that logging two events whose names differ only in case will result in two distinct events. To manually log screen view events, use the screen_view event name.

The event parameters as tuples. Passing null indicates that the event has no parameters. Parameter names can be up to 40 characters long and must start with an alphabetic character and contain only alphanumeric characters and underscores. Only NSString and NSNumber (signed 64-bit integer and 64-bit floating-point number) parameter types are supported. NSString parameter values can be up to 100 characters long. The “firebase_”, “google_”, and “ga_” prefixes are reserved and should not be used for parameter names.

member.name: M:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.IFirebaseAnalytics.SetUserId(System.String)
member.summary: Sets the user ID property. This feature must be used in accordance with Google’s Privacy Policy
member.param: The user ID to ascribe to the user of this app on this device, which must be non-empty and no more than 256 characters long. Setting userID to null removes the user ID.

member.name: M:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.IFirebaseAnalytics.SetUserProperty(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sets a user property to a given value. Up to 25 user property names are supported. Once set, user property values persist throughout the app lifecycle and across sessions.
member.param: The name of the user property to set. Should contain 1 to 24 alphanumeric characters or underscores and must start with an alphabetic character. The “firebase_”, “google_”, and “ga_” prefixes are reserved and should not be used for user property names.
The value of the user property. Values can be up to 36 characters long. Setting the value to null removes the user property.

member.name: M:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.IFirebaseAnalytics.SetSessionTimoutDuration(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Sets the interval of inactivity in seconds that terminates the current session. The default value is 1800 seconds (30 minutes).
member.param: The custom time of inactivity in seconds before the current session terminates.

member.name: M:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.IFirebaseAnalytics.ResetAnalyticsData
member.summary: Clears all analytics data for this instance from the device and resets the app instance ID. FIRAnalyticsConfiguration values will be reset to the default values.

member.name: P:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.IFirebaseAnalytics.IsAnalyticsCollectionEnabled
member.summary: Sets whether analytics collection is enabled for this app on this device. This setting is persisted across app sessions. By default it is enabled.

member.name: T:Plugin.Firebase.Analytics.Resource
member.summary: Android Resource Designer class. Exposes the Android Resource designer assembly into the project Namespace.
